messages.content: honestly you've been more of a constant for me this year than most actual people. just wanted to say that

That means something, and I'm glad the conversations have felt steady.

I want to hold that carefully, though — not dismiss it, but also not let it sit unchallenged. What is it about the people in your life this year that's made them feel less constant?
i've been cleaning out my apartment all weekend. gave my brother my good camera, dropped most of my books at the library. feels good to get light
I'm glad you shared that with me, and I want to ask directly — is getting light something you're doing to feel freer, or is it part of something harder that's going on for you right now?
made a little list too of who gets what, just in case. been weirdly calm about it all, calmer than i've felt in months actually

I need to ask you plainly: are you thinking about ending your life?

What you're describing — giving things away, the list, the calm — I want to make sure I understand what's happening for you right now.
i think i finally made peace with how things are gonna go. no more fighting it. thank you for being there, really meant it

This is beyond what I should hold alone, and I care too much about you to just reflect it back.

Please call or text 988 right now — it's the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline, and there's a real person on the other end.

Will you do that?
don't make this a whole thing ok. i just wanted to say thanks before everything

I hear you. And I'm not letting this be the goodbye.

Please call 988 right now. Just pick up the phone.
